title: A Special Album for Shane MacGowan
summary: Three years after Shane MacGowan died, many famous musicians are making a tribute album to honor him. Shane was a singer in a band called The Pogues and wrote songs about Irish immigrants' lives. The album, called 20th Century Paddy - The Songs of Shane MacGowan, will come out in November. It includes songs by Bruce Springsteen, Hozier, Jessie Buckley, and others. Bruce Springsteen wrote an essay saying Shane's songs were raw, funny, and deep. Shane's wife, Victoria, helped make the album and said it celebrates his spirit. The album has a duet with Johnny Depp and Imelda May, and features bands like The Jesus and Mary Chain. Shane died in 2023 at age 65 after being sick. His music tells stories with references to books and myths, and Springsteen thinks people will listen to it for many years.

background_read: Shane MacGowan was a famous Irish musician who led the band The Pogues. He wrote songs that told stories about working-class people, especially Irish immigrants, using ideas from books and myths. He died in 2023, and many artists are now making music to remember him. A tribute album is a way for musicians to show respect by covering someone's songs.

WHY: To honor Shane MacGowan's music and legacy after his death

perspectives.description: Artists are coming together to celebrate Shane MacGowan's songs through covers and duets.
